name: Pusher Beams Push Notifications API
what_it_does: Hosted push notification delivery API — abstracting Apple APNs and Google FCM behind a unified API for sending push notifications to iOS, Android, and web browsers with topic-based (interests) or device-specific targeting.
use_cases: Sending push notifications to mobile devices via unified API without managing APNs/FCM credentials
Broadcasting notifications to topic subscribers (interests) without maintaining device registrations
Sending user-targeted notifications with Pusher Beams authenticated user model
Web push notifications (browser) alongside mobile push from single API
Real-time alert delivery to agents' mobile apps or browser extensions
not_for: High-volume enterprise push at millions of devices (use Firebase FCM directly or OneSignal)
Rich push notifications with images, actions, and carousels (limited vs. direct APNs/FCM)
Marketing campaign push with advanced segmentation and A/B testing
best_when: A developer needs a simple unified API to send push notifications to iOS/Android/web without managing APNs and FCM credentials separately.
avoid_when: You need enterprise-scale push, rich notifications, marketing automation, or advanced segmentation.

auth.notes: Instance ID + Secret key pair. Server SDK authenticates with secret key. Client devices authenticate with instance ID. Authenticated users use JWT-based device authentication. Secret key server-side only.

pricing.notes: Free tier limited to 1,000 devices — sufficient for development and small apps. UK-based (Pusher, part of MessageBird ecosystem). Simple per-device pricing. Focused on developer experience. Part of Pusher product suite alongside Channels (websockets).

performance.rate_limits: Rate limits per instance; contact for specifics

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: Simple REST API for publish operations. Server SDKs for 7 languages. Interest-based and user-targeted delivery modes. No webhooks — delivery confirmation via device acknowledgment only. Good developer documentation with clear examples.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Notification publishes are new sends each time — not idempotent. Device interest subscriptions idempotent. Authenticated user assignment idempotent.
known_agent_gotchas: Free tier limited to 1,000 devices — production apps quickly exceed this
No delivery receipt webhooks — cannot confirm if device received notification
APNs/FCM credentials must be configured in Pusher dashboard — not API-configurable
Authenticated user model requires device-side JWT token generation before targeting by user

security_score_components.security_notes: TLS enforced. SOC2 certified. GDPR compliant. Server secret key for publishing. Device authentication via JWT prevents spoofing. US and EU data residency. APNs/FCM credentials stored encrypted. Part of MessageBird/Bird enterprise infrastructure.
